questions.q: Indian Parallel Cinema is best described as which kind of movement?
options: A movement devoted to mythological fantasy films
A movement focused only on silent-era comedies
A realist, socially engaged art-cinema movement
questions.explanation: Parallel Cinema was a movement of realist, socially engaged art cinema that emerged as an alternative to mainstream commercial filmmaking.

questions.q: Indian Parallel Cinema is generally said to have emerged as an alternative to which kind of filmmaking?
questions.explanation: Parallel Cinema arose as a deliberate alternative to India's dominant commercial film industry.

questions.q: Which government body is most associated with state funding of early Parallel Cinema in India?
questions.explanation: The Film Finance Corporation, set up in 1960, provided low-interest loans that funded many early Parallel Cinema films.

questions.q: Which Bengali filmmaker, who debuted with Pather Panchali (1955), is regarded as a foundational figure of Indian art cinema linked to the Parallel Cinema tradition?
questions.explanation: Satyajit Ray, who debuted with Pather Panchali in 1955, is a foundational figure of the realist Indian art-cinema tradition.

questions.q: Which director's 1974 film Ankur is often cited as a key work that opened up 1970s Parallel Cinema?
questions.explanation: Shyam Benegal's Ankur (1974) is widely cited as a landmark that opened up the 1970s Parallel Cinema wave.

questions.q: Which actress, who debuted in Shyam Benegal's Ankur (1974), became one of the most decorated performers associated with Parallel Cinema?
questions.explanation: Shabana Azmi debuted in Benegal's Ankur (1974) and became the most decorated performer associated with Parallel Cinema, winning five National Film Awards for Best Actress.

questions.q: Which actress, known for films such as Manthan (1976) and Bhumika (1977), is often called the 'conscience of Parallel Cinema'?
questions.explanation: Smita Patil, known for Manthan (1976) and Bhumika (1977), was a defining face of Parallel Cinema.

questions.q: Which premier government film school in Pune is closely associated with training many Parallel Cinema filmmakers and actors?
options: FTII (Film and Television Institute of India)
questions.explanation: FTII in Pune trained many Parallel Cinema figures, including Mani Kaul, Kumar Shahani, Shabana Azmi and Naseeruddin Shah.

questions.q: Which actor, an FTII graduate known for Nishant (1975) and Sparsh (1980), became a leading face of Parallel Cinema?
questions.explanation: Naseeruddin Shah, an FTII graduate, became a leading face of Parallel Cinema in films such as Nishant (1975) and Sparsh (1980).

questions.q: Parallel Cinema in India is often contrasted with which other broad category of films?
questions.explanation: Parallel Cinema is typically contrasted with mainstream commercial 'masala' films that blend action, romance, comedy and song-and-dance.

questions.q: Mrinal Sen's 1969 Hindi film, widely credited with helping launch the Indian New Wave linked to Parallel Cinema, was titled what?
questions.explanation: Mrinal Sen's Bhuvan Shome (1969) is widely credited with helping launch the Indian New Wave.

questions.q: In which decade did Indian Parallel Cinema chiefly begin to emerge as a distinct movement?
questions.explanation: Parallel Cinema emerged across India from the late 1960s as an alternative to commercial filmmaking.

questions.q: Bimal Roy's 1953 film about a farmer trying to save his land, influenced by Italian neorealism, is regarded as an early template for the Parallel Cinema movement. What is its title?
questions.explanation: Bimal Roy's Do Bigha Zamin (1953), influenced by Italian neorealism, became a template for the parallel film movement.

questions.q: Adoor Gopalakrishnan's debut feature Swayamvaram (1972) is regarded as the founding work of which regional offshoot of Parallel Cinema?
questions.explanation: Adoor Gopalakrishnan's Swayamvaram (1972) is regarded as the founding work of the Malayalam New Wave.

questions.q: Shyam Benegal's 1976 Parallel Cinema film about the dairy cooperative movement, partly financed by small contributions from Gujarat farmers, was titled what?
questions.explanation: Benegal's Manthan (1976), about the dairy cooperative movement, was financed by two-rupee contributions from around 500,000 Gujarat farmers.

questions.q: Into which organisation did the Film Finance Corporation merge in 1980, continuing state support for art and experimental cinema?
options: The Central Board of Film Certification
questions.explanation: In 1980 the Film Finance Corporation merged with another body to form the National Film Development Corporation (NFDC).

questions.q: Which Bengali director, known for Partition-haunted films like Meghe Dhaka Tara (1960), is considered a major figure of the art-cinema tradition that fed into Parallel Cinema?
questions.explanation: Ritwik Ghatak, known for Meghe Dhaka Tara (1960), is a major figure of the art-cinema tradition associated with Parallel Cinema.

questions.q: Which 1974 Hindi film by M. S. Sathyu, about a Muslim family in Agra after Partition, is considered a notable work of Parallel Cinema?
questions.explanation: M. S. Sathyu's Garm Hava (1974), about a Muslim family deciding whether to remain in India after Partition, is a notable Parallel Cinema film.

questions.q: The card on Parallel Cinema names which Kannada filmmaker among the movement's figures, alongside Ray, Sen, Benegal and Adoor?
questions.explanation: The card lists Girish Kasaravalli among the figures spanning the languages of Parallel Cinema.

questions.q: Which kind of recognition did Indian Parallel Cinema films most consistently earn, distinguishing them from purely commercial successes?
options: Long theatrical runs at single-screen theatres
questions.explanation: Parallel Cinema films earned sustained recognition at international film festivals rather than primarily commercial success.

questions.q: Which two early films are commonly cited as among the first projects funded by the Film Finance Corporation that helped seed Parallel Cinema?
questions.explanation: The Film Finance Corporation funded experimental films including Bhuvan Shome (1969) and Sara Akash (1969).

questions.q: Which actress associated with Parallel Cinema was the daughter of poet Kaifi Azmi and a graduate of FTII?
questions.explanation: Shabana Azmi, an FTII graduate and the daughter of poet Kaifi Azmi, was a central performer in Parallel Cinema.

questions.q: Which two FTII-trained filmmakers, both noted for highly formalist Parallel Cinema work, are commonly grouped together as pioneers of an austere art-cinema style?
questions.explanation: FTII alumni Mani Kaul and Kumar Shahani are commonly grouped as pioneers of austere, formalist Parallel Cinema.

questions.q: In which year was the Film Finance Corporation, a key state patron of early Parallel Cinema, established by the Government of India?
questions.explanation: The Film Finance Corporation was established by the Government of India in 1960.

questions.q: Shyam Benegal, called an architect of Parallel Cinema, directed which sequence of films that defined the realist Parallel Cinema of the 1970s?
options: Awaara, Shree 420, Boot Polish and Jagte Raho
Roja, Bombay, Dil Se and Guru
questions.explanation: Shyam Benegal directed Ankur (1974), Nishant (1975), Manthan (1976) and Bhumika (1977), defining 1970s Parallel Cinema.

questions.q: Which Bengali director, whose 1969 Hindi film helped launch the New Wave, was India's leading politically engaged auteur with films like Akaler Sandhane (1980)?
questions.explanation: Mrinal Sen, whose Bhuvan Shome (1969) helped launch the New Wave, was India's leading politically engaged auteur, with films like Akaler Sandhane (1980).

questions.q: Which co-founder of the Chitralekha Film Cooperative is regarded as the father of the Malayalam New Wave strand of Parallel Cinema?
questions.explanation: Adoor Gopalakrishnan, a co-founder of the Chitralekha Film Cooperative, is regarded as the father of the Malayalam New Wave.

questions.q: Apart from the Film Finance Corporation, which grassroots cultural institutions helped sustain Parallel Cinema, especially in regions like Kerala?
questions.explanation: Film societies and cooperatives helped sustain Parallel Cinema, particularly the realist movement in Kerala.

questions.q: Which 1972 National Award-winning film is most directly credited with launching the Malayalam strand of Parallel Cinema?
questions.explanation: Adoor Gopalakrishnan's Swayamvaram (1972), which won four National Film Awards, launched the Malayalam New Wave.

questions.q: Which actor associated with Parallel Cinema, an FTII graduate, co-founded the theatre group Motley and won three National Film Awards?
questions.explanation: Naseeruddin Shah, a three-time National Film Award winner, co-founded the theatre group Motley.
